channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
498
ABPZ20 KNHC 230524
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 PM PDT Wed Jul 22 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Hurricane
Fausto, located well west-southwest of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A trough of low pressure located several hundred miles south of the
coast of southern Mexico continues to produce a large area of
showers and thunderstorms. Environmental conditions are conducive
for gradual development of this system, and a tropical depression is
likely to form late this week into the weekend, while it moves
generally west-northwestward at 10 to 15 mph, remaining well
offshore of Mexico.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...medium...60 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...90 percent.

Well Southwest of Hawaii:
A trough of low pressure located well southwest of the Hawaiian
Islands is producing disorganized showers and thunderstorms.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system, and a tropical depression is likely to
form over the weekend or early next week while the system moves
slowly westward to west-northwestward. The system is forecast to
move into the western Pacific basin late this weekend or early next
week.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...20 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...70 percent.

&&
Products issued by the U.S. Joint Typhoon Warning Center can be
found at: www.metoc.navy.mil/jtwc/jtwc.html

Official tropical cyclone forecast information issued by the Japan
Meteorological Agency for the western Pacific basin can be found at:
www.jma.go.jp/jma/jma-eng/jma-center/rsmc-hp-pub-eg/RSMC_HP.htm

$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Hurricane Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO HOLDING STEADY... As of 11:00 PM HST Wed Jul 22 the center of Fausto was located near 17.8, -124.0 with movement WNW at 12 mph. The minimum central pressure was 982 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 80 mph.

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Public Advisory Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 1100 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026 
347 
WTPZ31 KNHC 230834
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Hurricane Fausto Advisory Number  18
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026
 
...FAUSTO HOLDING STEADY...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 1100 PM HST...0900 UTC...INFORMATION
-----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...17.8N 124.0W
ABOUT 980 MI...1575 KM WSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
ABOUT 2035 MI...3275 KM E OF HILO HAWAII
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...80 MPH...130 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 285 DEGREES AT 12 MPH...19 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...982 MB...29.00 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 1100 PM HST (0900 UTC), the center of Hurricane Fausto was
located near latitude 17.8 North, longitude 124.0 West. Fausto is
moving toward the west-northwest near 12 mph (19 km/h). A continued
west-northwestward to westward motion, along with an increase in
forward speed, is expected over the next several days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 80 mph (130 km/h) with higher 
gusts. Some fluctuations in intensity could occur over the next day 
or so but Fausto is forecast to gradually weaken thereafter.
 
Hurricane-force winds extend outward up to 45 miles (75 km) from the
center and tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 185 miles
(295 km).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 982 mb (29.00 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF: Swells generated by Fausto are affecting portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and are spreading northward
toward the coast of southern California. These swells are likely to
cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please
consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_ep1.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 500 AM HST.
 
$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
525 
WTPZ21 KNHC 230833
TCMEP1
 
HURRICANE FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  18
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
0900 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
HURRICANE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 17.8N 124.0W AT 23/0900Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 285 DEGREES AT  10 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  982 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  70 KT WITH GUSTS TO  80 KT.
64 KT....... 40NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT....... 70NE  40SE  10SW  50NW.
34 KT.......140NE 160SE  50SW 120NW.
4 M SEAS....210NE 165SE 135SW 210NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 17.8N 124.0W AT 23/0900Z
AT 23/0600Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 17.7N 123.5W
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 18.0N 126.0W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 70NE  40SE  10SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 140SE  60SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 18.3N 128.7W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 35NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  10SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 140SE  70SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z 18.6N 131.5W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 20NE   0SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 120SE  70SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0600Z 18.8N 134.2W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  20SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 110SE  70SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/1800Z 19.1N 136.7W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  40SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...120NE 100SE  60SW 110NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 26/0600Z 19.5N 139.3W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  30SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...120NE 100SE  60SW 100NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 27/0600Z 19.8N 144.2W
MAX WIND  60 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
50 KT... 30NE  20SE  10SW  30NW.
34 KT...100NE  80SE  40SW  90NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 28/0600Z 20.5N 149.5W
MAX WIND  55 KT...GUSTS  65 KT.
50 KT... 20NE  10SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT... 80NE  50SE  40SW  80NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 17.8N 124.0W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 23/1500Z
 
$$
FORECASTER KELLY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 1100 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026 
386 
WTPZ41 KNHC 230835
TCDEP1
 
Hurricane Fausto Discussion Number  18
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 PM HST Wed Jul 22 2026
 
Fausto's satellite appearance has become rather ragged since the 
previous advisory. Proxy-vis and infrared imagery show a ragged eye 
feature, with much of the convection on the western side of the 
system eroded by drier air. Cloud tops have started to warm as well. 
That being said, a 0600 UTC ASCAT-C scatterometer pass showed 
satellite-derived winds around 58 kt, though given the instrument's 
undersampling bias, peak winds are likely higher. Satellite 
intensity estimates from UW-CIMSS range from 53 to 68 kt, and the CI 
value from TAFB is T4.5/77 kt, although the data-T value did drop to 
T4.0/65 kt for this advisory. Taking all this into account, the 
initial intensity for this advisory is held at 70 kt, although that 
could be generous given the degrading satellite appearance.
 
The system has started to increase in forward speed and is moving at 
an estimated motion of 285/10 kt. Fausto should continue to move 
west-northwestward to westward along the southern edge of a 
strengthening ridge to the north of the system. The track model 
guidance is in fairly good agreement through day 3. Beyond that 
period, there is increasing along-track spread, with the GDMI and 
HCCA guidance farther south and much faster than the rest of the 
model guidance, with the GFS being the slowest and farther north. 
The difference is about 300 n mi between the GFS and GDMI solutions. 
The official NHC forecast continues to trend toward the faster HCCA 
corrected-consensus and GDMI model solutions.
 
Fausto will remain in a low-shear environment, tracking parallel to 
the 26C–27C isotherms. The primary inhibiting factor will be the dry 
air that lies ahead, with mid-level relative humidity values 
dropping below 50 percent in 2–3 days. The system has already been 
periodically ingesting dry air from the north and west over the last 
day or so. This will lead to a gradual weakening trend, and the 
intensity forecast is highly sensitive to how well Fausto can fend 
off dry air from intruding into the inner core. The NHC intensity 
forecast calls for gradual weakening throughout the period, although 
fluctuations in intensity are possible. This forecast lies near the 
higher end of the model guidance, as some models move the system 
farther north and weaken it more quickly. It should be noted any 
northward track deviations would cause Fausto to weaken even faster 
than currently forecast.

 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  23/0900Z 17.8N 124.0W   70 KT  80 MPH
 12H  23/1800Z 18.0N 126.0W   65 KT  75 MPH
 24H  24/0600Z 18.3N 128.7W   65 KT  75 MPH
 36H  24/1800Z 18.6N 131.5W   65 KT  75 MPH
 48H  25/0600Z 18.8N 134.2W   60 KT  70 MPH
 60H  25/1800Z 19.1N 136.7W   60 KT  70 MPH
 72H  26/0600Z 19.5N 139.3W   60 KT  70 MPH
 96H  27/0600Z 19.8N 144.2W   60 KT  70 MPH
120H  28/0600Z 20.5N 149.5W   55 KT  65 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 230834
PWSEP1
                                                                    
HURRICANE FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  18                
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
0900 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0900Z THE CENTER OF HURRICANE FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR LATITUDE   
17.8 NORTH...LONGITUDE 124.0 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS NEAR 
70 KTS...80 MPH...130 KM/H.                                         
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        06Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN 06Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN 06Z MON 06Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
20N 125W       34 14   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)
 
20N 130W       34  X  23(23)  35(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)   X(58)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   7( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   1( 1)  42(43)  22(65)   X(65)   X(65)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)  10(10)  13(23)   X(23)   X(23)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   4( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  50(50)  18(68)   X(68)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  17(17)  14(31)   X(31)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)   6(11)   X(11)
 
20N 145W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  38(38)  14(52)
20N 145W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  12(12)   9(21)
20N 145W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   2( 6)
 
20N 150W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  27(27)
20N 150W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   8( 8)
20N 150W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)
 
20N 151W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  19(19)
20N 151W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)
 
BUOY 51004     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
20N 154W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER KELLY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Graphics
